data.title: SoFi Stock Is Trending Lower Today. Is It a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Shares of SoFi Technologies (SOFI) were trending lower in the premarket action. The fintech firm is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 results on Friday, January 30. Despite today's weakness, the stock has rallied about 46% in 2025, easily outperforming the broader market. SoFi has delivered strong results in recent quarters, driven by steady growth in its member base and improving operating performance. That said, while several analysts point to SoFi's solid execution and growth outlook, some remain cautious due to the stock's elevated valuation. As a result, Wall Street's consensus rating reflects a more cautious stance heading into the Q4 earnings report.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What to Expect from SoFi on January 30

Wall Street analysts expect Q4 revenue to slip about 3.4% to roughly $973.42 million. Meanwhile, earnings are also expected to be lower, with EPS forecast at $0.11, down from $0.29 in the same quarter last year.

Investors will be watching for updates on loan growth, credit quality, and guidance when the company reports its earnings.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: TSMC Ramps Up AI Chip Packaging While Nvidia Controls Most 2026 Capacity
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) is moving quickly to expand its advanced chip packaging operations in Taiwan, as this work has become just as important as chip manufacturing. The company is adding capacity at its Chiayi AP7 site and upgrading tools at its Longtan AP3 plant, based on reports from TrendForce and local media.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a result, industry estimates suggest TSMC's CoWoS capacity could reach about 125,000 to 130,000 wafers per month by the end of 2026. Meanwhile, TSMC plans to open its Chiayi AP7 facility to reporters on January 22, marking the first time this advanced packaging site will be shown to the media.

On its recent earnings call, TSMC Chief Financial Officer Wendell Huang said advanced packaging made up about 8% of the company's revenue in 2025 and should rise above 10% in 2026. He also stated that 10% to 20% of TSMC's planned $52 billion to $56 billion in capital spending for 2026 will go toward packaging, testing, and related work.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Why Is Wall Street Bullish on UnitedHealth Stock (UNH) Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
UnitedHealth (UNH) has declined 35% over the past year due to several headwinds, including higher medical costs in the health insurer's Medicare Advantage (MA) business and a Department of Justice investigation into the company's billing practices. Moreover, an investigation by a Senate Committee recently revealed that UNH used aggressive tactics to increase U.S. government reimbursement for patients enrolled in the MA healthcare plans. Despite these pressures, most Wall Street analysts remain bullish on UNH stock ahead of the company's Q4 results scheduled to be announced on January 27.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Despite ongoing challenges, analysts are positive about management's ability to revive UNH's business and improve the company's profitability over the long term.

Notably, Wall Street expects UnitedHealth to report EPS (earnings per share) of $2.12 for Q4 2025, reflecting a 69% year-over-year decline. Revenue is expected to grow by about 13% to $113.8 billion.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Falls Despite Key Catalysts
data.description:
Shares of electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla (TSLA) fell in pre-market trading amid a broader market decline, despite several positive catalysts. President Donald Trump threatened new tariffs on eight European allies over his demand to acquire Greenland, rattling global markets. Futures on the three major U.S. indexes plunged on the news.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

U.S. markets remained closed for the extended Martin Luther King Jr. holiday on Monday, but Tesla had multiple developments poised to lift shares at Tuesday's (January 20) open.

Key Catalysts That Could Affect Tesla Stock

  1. Canada Reopens EV Market: Canada announced a new trade deal that lowers tariffs on China-made EVs, reopening its market after a 2024 shutdown. Tesla stands to gain as this allows limited imports of China-built Teslas at reduced rates, easing access to a key sales region.
  2. Cybercab Prototype: A Tesla Cybercab prototype spotted in Chicago had a rear camera washer, a feature long-requested by owners. This addresses issues related to dirty lenses from snow, slush, or weather that impair autonomous driving cameras. The visible washer hints Tesla may equip other exterior cameras similarly.
  3. Dojo 3 Restart: Tesla is restarting its Dojo 3 supercomputer project after AI5 chip advances cleared obstacles. Elon Musk confirmed the news on social media on Sunday. Success could enhance Tesla's AI for autonomy and robotics, boosting long-term stock appeal beyond EVs. Dojo 3 is Tesla's in-house AI supercomputer, designed to train massive neural networks using the company's own AI5 chips.
  4. South Korea Price Cuts: Tesla is aggressively cutting prices in South Korea to gain sales and share in the key Asian market. Model 3 Standard RWD prices dropped to 41.99 million won ($28,500) from 59.9 million won. With subsidies, final costs fall into the low-30-million-won range, undercutting rivals like BYD (BYDDF), Hyundai, Kia, BMW, and Mercedes (typically 40-50 million won).

Is Tesla Stock Worth Buying?

Analysts remain sidelined on Tesla. On TipRanks Tesla shares have a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $397.47 implies 9.2% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA stock has gained 3.2%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/20/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Plunge amid Trump's Greenland Tariff Threat
data.description:
U.S. stock futures dropped early Tuesday, starting the holiday-shortened trading week on a negative note. President Donald Trump has threatened a new round of tariffs over his demand to acquire Greenland, rattling global markets. U.S. markets were closed on Monday in honor of the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.76%, 1.51%, and 1.38%, respectively, at 3:51 a.m. EST on January 20.

Last week, all three major averages closed lower, despite the rally in semiconductor stocks on Thursday after Taiwan Semiconductor's (TSM) blockbuster results. The S&P 500 declined 0.4%, the DJIA fell 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.7%.

In a Saturday post on Truth Social, Trump announced a 10% U.S. tariff on eight European allies, including the UK, effective February 1. The tariffs will eventually rise to 25% on June 1 and will stay in place until the U.S. strikes a deal to acquire Greenland.

Looking forward, investors will focus on Trump's speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos on Wednesday, where he will discuss the Greenland issue.

The earnings season also remains in focus. Notable companies reporting this week include Netflix (NFLX), United Airlines (UAL), 3M (MMM), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Charles Schwab (SCHW), Intel (INTC), and Visa (V).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.28%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.15 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,721 per ounce on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower today amid Trump's renewed tariff threats. European countries are reportedly discussing counter-tariffs and wider economic penalties in response.

Moreover, this morning, Trump threatened 200% tariffs on French wine and champagne after reports stated that France's President Macron refused to join his "Board of Peace" on Gaza.

Asia-Pacific Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific markets fell on January 20 due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, including those involving Iran and Greenland.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.29%. In China, the Shanghai Composite slipped 0.01%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.52%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei dropped 1.11%, and the Topix declined 0.84%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Here are Three Stocks Celebrating Anniversary of Trump 2.0
data.description:
Let's look at three stocks celebrating the anniversary of the start of Trump 2.0. For some people it might feel like only yesterday, while for others it's been more like a lifetime in the Upside Down. Today – January 20th – marks the one year anniversary of President Trump's inauguration as the 47th leader of the free world.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

His return for a second spell in office has been rather "colorful" – tariffs, military attacks on Iran, deportations, ICE, ceasefire negotiations in Ukraine and Gaza, a government shutdown, a criminal probe into the Federal Reserve and the capture of a foreign leader.

Trump's Testing Period

"It's been a testing period for consumers and businesses to put it mildly, amid extraordinary levels of unpredictable behaviour by the president which have had consequences globally," said Dan Coatsworth, head of markets at AJ Bell. "Trump's actions over the past year have, or stand to have, a profound impact on consumers, businesses, geopolitics, global trade, and more."

Some stocks have flourished during the period and other stocks have floundered. Let's look at the stocks which will be hanging out the red, white and blue in celebration.

Gold Miners

Barrick Mining (B) – Its share price has rocketed over 200% higher in the last twelve months. The main driver has been the surge in investor demand for gold as they seek a safe haven in a world of greater political and economic insecurity. Retail investors have helped gold ETF inflows like the SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD) with central banks also scooping up more of the precious metal to bolster their reserves in difficult times. Trump's policies have also weakened the U.S. dollar and led to sticky inflation which also makes gold look more attractive.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: 3 Best Value Stocks with Over 30% Upside in 2026, According to Analysts
data.description:
Investors looking for undervalued stocks in the current volatile market may want to pay close attention to Wall Street's latest picks. These stocks stand out for their solid fundamentals, attractive valuations, and catalysts that could drive meaningful gains in the months ahead.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Before we go further, let's define value stocks. These are companies that look undervalued compared to their true worth, trading at lower price-to-earnings or price-to-book ratios. When the market eventually recognizes their real value, they can deliver strong gains.

Now, let's check the three value stocks that analysts have spotlighted. Each carries a "Strong Buy" consensus rating but also offers more than 20% upside potential from current levels.

Here Are This Week's Stocks

Abbott Laboratories (ABT) – This healthcare company is known for developing medical devices, diagnostics, and nutritional products. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $150.07, implying a 23.25% upside potential from the current levels. The company's P/E of 15.27x reflects a 44.2% discount to the Healthcare sector's median of 27.35.

The company will release its Q4 results on January 22. Abbott is expected to report revenues of $11.80 billion on earnings of $1.50 per share.

Carnival (CCL) – Carnival operates multiple cruise line brands across global destinations. Its average price target of $37.93 implies a 31.15% upside potential from the current levels. CCL stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating. Trading at a P/E of 14.46x, the company is valued 29.6% below the Consumer Cyclical sector's median multiple of 20.54.

Uber Technologies (UBER) – Uber provides ride-hailing and food and package delivery services. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $113.76, implying a 34.07% upside potential from the current levels. With a P/E ratio of 10.89x, the stock is priced at a 19.5% discount to the Technology sector's median of 13.52.

Recently, KeyBanc analyst Justin Patterson cut his price target on Uber stock to $105 from $110 but kept a Buy rating, citing strong adoption across ridesharing and delivery and a generally positive outlook.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Netflix Stock Forecast: Blockbuster Content Could Be Sidelined by the 'Ongoing Circus' of Warner Bros. Merger
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) is entering an important earnings print today as it tries to prove that its business is still healthy despite the chaos of its $82.7 billion bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). While the headlines are dominated by a "messy" bidding war involving rivals like Paramount Skydance (PSKY), investors are looking for proof that the streaming leader can still grow on its own. The stock has plummeted 29% since October, erasing nearly a year of gains, as the market weighs the massive price tag of the Warner deal against slowing subscriber numbers.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Netflix's Core Business Is Still a Powerhouse

Despite the merger noise, many analysts believe Netflix's core business is still a powerhouse. The company recently released the finale of Stranger Things, hosted a major Jake Paul boxing match, and streamed NFL games on Christmas. These events are expected to drive solid fourth-quarter results.

  • Earnings Growth: Wall Street expects earnings of 55 cents per share, which would be a 28% increase compared to last year.
  • Subscriber Beats: Some analysts predict Netflix added over 14 million new paid users, beating the average market expectation of 11 million.

The Biggest Threat Is the "Circus" of the Warner Deal

The biggest threat to the stock price aren't the earnings, it's the potential $82.7 billion bill for Warner Bros. Discovery. Some investors worry that Netflix is overpaying for a legacy media company, especially since Netflix has traditionally grown without making big acquisitions. Benchmark analyst Daniel Kurnos noted that "there is no chance that this print distracts investors from the ongoing circus" surrounding the merger. This uncertainty has turned some former fans of the stock into bears who are waiting to "short" the shares if the price pops after the report.

The Bidding War Gets Messier

Regarding the latest merger updates, Netflix is reportedly changing its strategy to beat out Paramount Skydance. Reports suggest Netflix is now considering an all-cash offer to speed up the process. While this might help them win the deal, it would also put a massive strain on the company's balance sheet. If the deal fails, some investors might actually celebrate, as it would remove the risk of a "poor transaction track record" that often haunts big media mergers.

Analysts Expect a "Banner Year" for Connected Television

Looking ahead, the company is leaning on new advertising partnerships, including a deal with Amazon (AMZN), to keep revenue moving upward. While analysts expect revenue growth to slow down slightly throughout most of 2026, they anticipate a "banner year" for connected television starting in 2027. Investors will be watching the guidance, the company's own prediction for the future, to see if the management team still believes in their organic growth levers or if they feel forced to buy Warner just to stay ahead.

Is Netflix a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Netflix stock based on 26 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average NFLX stock price target of $126.33 indicates 43.6% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani
